title: Derris elliptica
taxonomy: Derris elliptica is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. Derris elliptica is a shrub or small tree, and its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Fabales, Family: Fabaceae, Genus: Derris, Species: D. elliptica. The plant is also known by its common name, Derris or Toba. This plant is part of the Derris genus, which is used as a source for rotenone, a natural insecticide.
uses: Derris elliptica, also known as the poisonous bean or Derris, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots contain rotenone, a toxic compound with insecticidal properties that have been used to treat certain medical conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots are also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections. However, its use requires caution due to the toxicity of rotenone.
benefits: Derris elliptica, also known as the kudzu plant, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its extracts have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Studies suggest that Derris elliptica may help in reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, its extracts have been found to possess antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits and potential applications.
properties: Derris elliptica is traditionally used in medicine for its anthelmintic and insecticidal properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various parasitic infections. Its roots contain a range of bioactive compounds, including rotenone and deguelin, which have been found to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. These compounds have been studied for their potential in treating various diseases, including cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. Derris elliptica's extracts have also been investigated for their antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties, which may provide benefits in the treatment of infections. Furthermore, its use in traditional medicine has been associated with improved wound healing and reduced inflammation in some cases.
constituents: Derris elliptica contains a range of active constituents, primarily rotenone and derrisone, which are responsible for its insecticidal and piscicidal properties. These compounds have been isolated from the plant's roots and are known for their potent effects on insects and fish. Rotenone is a well-studied compound with a long history of use as an insecticide, while derrisone has been reported to have similar properties. The exact composition and concentration of these active constituents can vary depending on factors such as the plant's age, growing conditions, and extraction methods. Further research is needed to fully understand the complex chemistry of Derris elliptica.
parts: Derris elliptica is a plant species commonly known as the 'killing stick' or 'thorny pea'. Derris elliptica contains the medicinal part of the plant in the roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for their insecticidal and antiseptic properties. The roots of Derris elliptica contain rotenone, a toxic compound that has been used to make insecticides. The roots are also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The use of Derris elliptica roots in traditional medicine has been documented in many Asian cultures.
preparations: Derris elliptica is a plant species traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies in various cultures, particularly in Asia and Africa. The plant's root and bark are often used to create teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes. In traditional herbalism, Derris elliptica is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are thought to aid in wound healing and the treatment of various health conditions. However, the efficacy and safety of these herbal preparations are not extensively scientifically studied and validated. As a result, Derris elliptica herbal preparations should be used with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
side_effects: Derris elliptica is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but its use can have side effects.  It can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain in some individuals.  Some people may also experience headaches and dizziness after consuming Derris elliptica.  In severe cases, it can lead to seizures and even death due to its toxic nature.  The plant contains toxic compounds that can be hazardous if ingested in large quantities or without proper medical supervision.
intro: Derris elliptica, also known as the common Derris or sea island bean, is a perennial plant native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, including antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various diseases such as malaria, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Derris elliptica include rotenone, a naturally occurring insecticide, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
